qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed internal secondary-index key injection crashes indexed queries
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding crashes indexed queries via malformed `__oxia/idx/*` parsing in `secondary_indexes.go`, not the advisory's `KeepAlive()` / `heartbeat()` session race in `session_manager.go` and `session.go`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public version override bypasses expected-version concurrency checks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding abuses public `override_version_id` handling in `db.applyPut` and the write path; it does not involve session heartbeat processing, session closure, or channel-close races.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's server-crash root cause: a race between `KeepAlive()` lookup/locking and `session.heartbeat()` sending on a channel that may be full or closed during concurrent session shutdown.
evidence: The advisory describes a session-lifecycle bug in `oxiad/dataserver/controller/lead/session.go` and `session_manager.go`, specifically blocking or panicking heartbeat channel sends during concurrent `close()`.

The `public_internal_index_panic_dos` finding terminates in `secondaryIndexListIterator.Key()` parse panic after attacker-written `__oxia/idx/*` input; its exploit path uses indexed `List`/`RangeScan`, not `CreateSession`, `KeepAlive`, or `CloseSession`.

The `public_version_override_cas_bypass` finding terminates in `db.applyPut` version-override handling on `Write`/`WriteStream`; it is an integrity flaw unrelated to session heartbeats or session removal races.

The session checklist in `artifacts/component_family_checklist.md` explicitly cleared public session/streaming surfaces as not yielding a stronger validated finding, while the accepted internal-key availability finding was the separate secondary-index panic path.
